mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.33% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals continued monetary tightening, which increases the discount rate applied to long-duration assets and pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market reprices duration risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS is in tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 726,283 million dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,321 thousand units indicate robust consumer demand. Sustained strength in these demand proxies signals economic resilience, reducing flight-to-safety flows and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,200 thousand units
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days when both RSAFS and HOUST trigger conditions are met, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.2% is near cyclical lows. A further decline in UNRATE below 4.0% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, increasing inflation expectations and pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in higher future inflation.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines when UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.99 (index 2017=100) reflects moderate supply-side capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 103.0 signals supply-side weakness and potential deflationary pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth expectations decline and safe-haven demand increases.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days when INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in supply-contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation at 322.13 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.33% creates a real federal funds rate near 2.0%. If CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains fixed, the real rate compresses, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces term premium and pushes 10-year Treasury yields downward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2+ months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when both trigger conditions are met, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 23,685.3 billion chained 2017 dollars represents solid economic growth. A quarter-over-quarter deceleration in GDPC1 growth below 2.0% annualized signals weakening demand momentum, reducing inflation expectations and pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as growth concerns dominate.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over 60 trading days when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 159,539 thousand persons shows solid job creation. A sharp deceleration in month-over-month PAYEMS growth below 100,000 jobs combined with rising UNRATE signals labor market deterioration, triggering safe-haven flows that push 10-year Treasury yields downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100,000 AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when both labor market weakness triggers are met, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,321 thousand units represent elevated residential investment. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1,100 thousand units signals housing demand collapse, reducing construction employment and consumer wealth, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields downward as growth expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,100 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 5 basis points over 5 trading days when HOUST falls below 1,100 for 2 consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in housing contraction regimes.
